definition: The intending or volitional aspect of mind that directs action.
notes: Intention is the clearest default in contemporary English and works well across ethical and dependent-arising contexts. Volition remains acceptable in analytical prose, but it can sound more abstract than the project generally prefers.

context_rules.context: In most ethical and psychological contexts
In technical analytical prose about mental factors
context_rules.notes: Acceptable when the terminology needs to stay more formal.

example_phrases.translation: bhikkhus, I say that intention is action

example_phrases.notes: Headword form often stands clearly on its own in analytical lists.
